capital.summary: Temple University's Lewis Katz School of Medicine and AtlantiCare are partnering to establish a new regional medical college campus in Atlantic City, New Jersey, backed by a $50 million investment.
capital.timeline: Expected to open in August 2029 with an anticipated enrollment of 40 students per year.
capital.why_it_matters: This new medical school will require a complete fit-out of teaching and research laboratories, creating significant demand for a wide range of lab supplies, reagents, consumables, and scientific equipment.

breakthrough.summary: Researchers at Temple University's Lewis Katz School of Medicine, in collaboration with The Wistar Institute, have uncovered a previously unrecognized metabolic pathway in aggressive ovarian cancer cells. This pathway, involving αKG-mediated carnitine synthesis, helps cancer cells repair DNA damage and survive chemotherapy treatment, opening the door to new therapeutic strategies.
breakthrough.department_or_lab: Aging + Cardiovascular Discovery Center, Department of Cardiovascular Sciences, Lewis Katz School of Medicine at Temple University
breakthrough.award_or_discovery: Discovery of αKG-mediated carnitine synthesis driving DNA repair and chemotherapy resistance in ovarian cancer
breakthrough.significance: This is a first-in-class discovery, described as uncovering a "completely unexpected connection between cellular metabolism and DNA repair" and a "new piece of biology that nobody had described before", published in the prestigious journal Nature.
breakthrough.why_it_matters: This breakthrough in understanding cancer metabolism and resistance mechanisms will drive demand for advanced reagents, cell culture supplies, and analytical equipment for further research and drug development.

ai_summary: Temple University is significantly expanding its medical education and research capabilities with a new $50M regional medical college campus, creating substantial demand for new lab equipment and supplies. Concurrently, researchers made a breakthrough in ovarian cancer metabolism, signaling active, high-impact research that will drive ongoing needs for specialized reagents and instruments for cancer and drug discovery labs.
